claim_policy.reason: These pages are allowed to show finite evidence, proof gates, and candidate strategies, but must not claim a proof until an independently checkable infinite argument exists.

problems.title: Riemann Hypothesis
problems.target_statement: All non-trivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function have real part 1/2.
problems.tool_position: PrimeProject can test prime-counting error signatures and RH-compatible envelopes on finite ranges; it cannot turn finite checks into a proof.

certificate.statement: Prime-counting diagnostics are exactly recomputed at decimal checkpoints up to 1000000.
certificate.leaf_encoding: utf8 text, sha256 leaf, pairwise sha256 Merkle chunks

proof_attempt.attack_route: Prime-counting residuals -> explicit theta(x) envelope -> zero-free critical-strip control strong enough for RH.

known_theorem_bridges.name: Prime-counting error equivalences
known_theorem_bridges.role: Identify an explicit error envelope strong enough to imply RH.

known_theorem_bridges.name: Zero-free region machinery
known_theorem_bridges.role: Convert analytic estimates into critical-strip zero control.

lemma_candidates.statement: There exists an explicit C such that |theta(x)-x| <= C sqrt(x) log(x)^2 for all x >= x0.
lemma_candidates.evidence: Finite checkpoints are below the normalized envelope through the committed limit.
lemma_candidates.required_upgrade: Prove the inequality for the infinite tail and show constants meet an RH-equivalent bound.

obligations.claim: Bounded theta/pi diagnostics are exactly reproducible.
obligations.failure_mode: Merkle root mismatch or changed checkpoint row.

obligations.claim: Find an explicit residual bound valid for all x beyond a finite threshold.
obligations.failure_mode: A residual spike violates the proposed global envelope.

obligations.claim: Show the global envelope implies all non-trivial zeros lie on the critical line.
obligations.verifier: formal theorem or accepted analytic derivation
obligations.failure_mode: The envelope is weaker than a known RH-equivalent condition.

falsification_targets: A proposed all-x theta bound fails on a computed checkpoint.
The bound does not imply a published RH-equivalent criterion.
The argument depends on a finite zero table without an infinite tail theorem.

next_formalization_target.statement: For every non-trivial zero rho of zeta, Re(rho) = 1/2.

proof_attempt.promotion_rule: A page may move from open_not_proven only when every open obligation is replaced by an independently checkable proof that does not depend on the search limit.

proof_gates: Replace finite prime-counting evidence with a theorem controlling zeta zeros on the full critical strip.
Prove an explicit equivalence strong enough to imply all non-trivial zeros lie on Re(s)=1/2.
Show every numerical or symbolic step is independent of bounded search limits.

formal_proof_contract.lean_statement: theorem primeproject_riemann_hypothesis : forall rho, Zeta.NontrivialZero rho -> rho.re = (1/2 : Real) := by
forbidden_assumptions: any axiom implying all non-trivial zeta zeros have real part 1/2
acceptance_rules: No `sorry`, `admit`, unchecked axiom, or imported theorem equivalent to the target conjecture.
Every finite computation must be represented by a bounded certificate and every infinite step by a formal theorem.
The proof-status gate must report eligible_for_independent_review before any page can change from open_not_proven.
An external reviewer must be able to replay the kernel check without trusting PrimeProject code.
formal_proof_contract.current_blocker: open infinite obligations remain outside the proof assistant kernel

proof_milestone_queue.decisive_next_task: Formalize an explicit all-x prime-counting error theorem strong enough to connect finite theta diagnostics to an RH-equivalent bridge.

milestones.title: Bounded theta/pi certificate
milestones.exit_criterion: scripts/verify_open_problem_workbench.py reproduces the committed root.

milestones.title: Lean definitions for zeta zeros and theta diagnostics
milestones.exit_criterion: Kernel-checkable definitions compile without conjectural axioms.

milestones.title: All-x theta error theorem
milestones.exit_criterion: A theorem removes the finite search limit with explicit constants.

milestones.title: RH-equivalence bridge
milestones.exit_criterion: The all-x estimate implies every non-trivial zero lies on the critical line.

milestones.title: Independent kernel review
milestones.exit_criterion: A clean Lean replay passes outside PrimeProject.

proof_milestone_queue.promotion_rule: Only complete milestones backed by replayable artifacts can reduce the open_count. Heuristic evidence cannot close a milestone.

decisive_lemma_lab.decisive_question: Can finite theta residual discipline be upgraded into an explicit all-x bound strong enough for an RH-equivalent bridge?
decisive_lemma_lab.candidate_statement: There are explicit constants C and x0 such that |theta(x)-x| <= C sqrt(x) log(x)^2 for every x >= x0, and the constants satisfy a formal RH-equivalent criterion.
decisive_lemma_lab.proof_obligation: Replace checkpoint agreement with a theorem over all real x beyond a stated threshold and prove the equivalence bridge without importing RH.
decisive_lemma_lab.falsification_test: Search for a computed theta residual that violates the proposed envelope, then reject any envelope that fails to imply a known RH-equivalent statement.

automated_falsification_probe.proof_gap: Checkpoint probes do not quantify every x and do not prove the RH-equivalence bridge.

gaps.description: Checkpoint control must become an all-x theta estimate with explicit constants.
gaps.next_experiment: Fit explicit theta-envelope candidates from certified checkpoints, then stress them against denser local theta samples before attempting an all-x theorem.
gaps.failure_signal: A denser certified checkpoint violates the proposed envelope or the constants cannot be stated independently of the search limit.

gaps.description: The all-x estimate must imply an accepted RH-equivalent zero-control criterion.
gaps.next_experiment: Map each candidate theta bound to a published RH-equivalent or insufficient prime-counting criterion.
gaps.failure_signal: The best available bound is weaker than every accepted RH-equivalent criterion.

gaps.description: Zeta zero, theta, and explicit-bound objects must be kernel-checkable without conjectural imports.
gaps.next_experiment: Create a no-sorry Lean namespace containing only definitions and theorem statements for zeros, theta, and the proposed envelope.
gaps.failure_signal: The formal statement needs an imported theorem equivalent to RH or an unchecked axiom.

gaps.description: An external replay must verify the proof without trusting PrimeProject code.
gaps.next_experiment: Package the formal artifacts and verifier command once G1-G3 have closed.
gaps.failure_signal: A clean environment cannot replay the kernel check from published artifacts.

proof_gap_taxonomy.closure_rule: All proof gaps must be closed by formal proof artifacts or accepted theorem references before the conjecture page can change status.

decisive_lemma_lab.current_result: The bounded probe is compatible with the lemma but does not prove it.
decisive_lemma_lab.next_action: Formalize the target inequality and enumerate which published equivalence theorem would be sufficient.
decisive_lemma_lab.promotion_rule: The lab may upgrade a milestone only when the proof obligation is discharged by a formal theorem or accepted external proof, not by finite probe success.

candidate_strategy: Use generator-fingerprint residuals to look for structured departures from PNT-scale noise.
Convert any stable residual law into a falsifiable analytic lemma before treating it as proof evidence.
Reject any argument that only rephrases finite zero or finite prime-count checks.
problems.claim_boundary: No proof claim. Current evidence is only finite RH-compatible diagnostics.

proof_status_gate.machine_rule: A full-proof claim is blocked unless certificate status is bounded_theorem_certified and every obligation, graph link, theorem bridge, and lemma candidate is formal_proof_verified or accepted_theorem.

proof_execution_protocol.current_frontier: Formalize an explicit all-x prime-counting error theorem strong enough to connect finite theta diagnostics to an RH-equivalent bridge.
proof_execution_protocol.primary_next_experiment: Fit explicit theta-envelope candidates from certified checkpoints, then stress them against denser local theta samples before attempting an all-x theorem.
proof_execution_protocol.primary_failure_signal: A denser certified checkpoint violates the proposed envelope or the constants cannot be stated independently of the search limit.

stages.name: bounded certificate replay
stages.input: finite computation rows
stages.proof_value: Closes only the finite range; it does not discharge an infinite conjecture.

stages.name: formal target normalization
stages.input: Lean-oriented theorem statement and forbidden assumptions
stages.required_output: kernel-checkable definitions with no sorry/admit/target-equivalent axiom
stages.proof_value: Prevents finite evidence or conjectural imports from being mistaken for proof.

stages.name: decisive lemma attack
stages.input: There are explicit constants C and x0 such that |theta(x)-x| <= C sqrt(x) log(x)^2 for every x >= x0, and the constants satisfy a formal RH-equivalent criterion.
stages.required_output: Replace checkpoint agreement with a theorem over all real x beyond a stated threshold and prove the equivalence bridge without importing RH.
stages.verifier: formal theorem or accepted external theorem
stages.proof_value: The first infinite step that would close the main open milestones.

stages.name: gap work-order falsification
stages.input: Fit explicit theta-envelope candidates from certified checkpoints, then stress them against denser local theta samples before attempting an all-x theorem.
stages.verifier: A denser certified checkpoint violates the proposed envelope or the constants cannot be stated independently of the search limit.
stages.proof_value: Turns the next research move into a testable success/failure condition.

stages.name: full proof promotion gate
stages.input: all obligations, graph links, theorem bridges, lemma candidates, and formal contract
stages.proof_value: Blocks any public full-proof claim until every infinite bridge is independently checkable.

proof_execution_protocol.promotion_rule: The conjecture page cannot claim solved status until every protocol stage is complete or formally verified and the proof-status gate is eligible_for_independent_review.

problems.title: Collatz Conjecture
problems.target_statement: Repeatedly applying n/2 for even n and 3n+1 for odd n eventually reaches 1 for every positive integer.
problems.tool_position: PrimeProject can exhaustively replay trajectories over a bounded range and search for divergent behavior; bounded replay is not a proof over all integers.

certificate.statement: Every start value 1 <= n <= 1000000 reaches 1 under the Collatz map.
certificate.leaf_encoding: utf8 text, sha256 leaf, pairwise sha256 Merkle chunks

proof_attempt.attack_route: Odd-only accelerated map -> residue-block descent certificate -> recursive coverage of every positive integer.

known_theorem_bridges.name: Accelerated odd Collatz map
known_theorem_bridges.role: Reduce even divisions while preserving every trajectory branch.

known_theorem_bridges.name: Descent certificate induction
known_theorem_bridges.role: Turn residue-block certificates into a global well-founded descent argument.

lemma_candidates.statement: Every odd residue block modulo 2^a 3^b has a finite accelerated path to a smaller representative in a covered block.
lemma_candidates.evidence: Bounded replay identifies many descending trajectories and worst stopping-time cases.
lemma_candidates.required_upgrade: Construct a finite symbolic block cover with an induction measure that cannot increase forever.

obligations.claim: Every n <= 1,000,000 reaches 1 under the Collatz map.
obligations.failure_mode: A trajectory leaf changes or a non-reaching start value appears.

obligations.claim: Construct residue-block descent certificates that cover all sufficiently large blocks.
obligations.failure_mode: An uncovered block has no guaranteed descent path.

obligations.claim: Rule out divergent trajectories and non-trivial cycles simultaneously.
obligations.failure_mode: A cycle or non-descending infinite branch remains possible.

falsification_targets: A residue block cannot be assigned a monotone descent certificate.
A proposed drift inequality admits an infinite exceptional set.
Odd-only compression silently loses a valid trajectory branch.

next_formalization_target.statement: For every positive integer n, some iterate of the Collatz map reaches 1.

proof_attempt.promotion_rule: A page may move from open_not_proven only when every open obligation is replaced by an independently checkable proof that does not depend on the search limit.

proof_gates: Prove descent or recurrence for every congruence class without relying on finite enumeration.
Rule out non-trivial cycles and divergent trajectories simultaneously.
Turn stochastic drift intuition into a deterministic inequality with no exceptional infinite set.
candidate_strategy: Compress trajectories by odd-only maps and residue-class transitions.
Search for monotone certificates on blocks, then try to lift them to all blocks recursively.
Treat random-walk drift evidence as heuristic until a deterministic certificate exists.

formal_proof_contract.lean_statement: theorem primeproject_collatz_conjecture : forall n : Nat, 0 < n -> exists k, Collatz.iterate k n = 1 := by
required_artifacts: formal cycle and divergence exclusion theorem
forbidden_assumptions: well_foundedness of Collatz trajectories without proof
any axiom excluding non-trivial cycles or divergent paths
acceptance_rules: No `sorry`, `admit`, unchecked axiom, or imported theorem equivalent to the target conjecture.
Every finite computation must be represented by a bounded certificate and every infinite step by a formal theorem.
The proof-status gate must report eligible_for_independent_review before any page can change from open_not_proven.
An external reviewer must be able to replay the kernel check without trusting PrimeProject code.
formal_proof_contract.current_blocker: open infinite obligations remain outside the proof assistant kernel

proof_milestone_queue.decisive_next_task: Construct a symbolic residue-block descent certificate that covers an infinite family, not only enumerated starts.

milestones.title: Bounded trajectory certificate
milestones.exit_criterion: Every start value through the committed limit replays to 1.

milestones.title: Lean definitions for accelerated Collatz dynamics
milestones.exit_criterion: The odd-only map is proved equivalent to the ordinary trajectory relation.

milestones.title: Residue-block descent theorem
milestones.exit_criterion: Every sufficiently large block descends to a smaller covered representative.

milestones.title: Cycle and divergence exclusion
milestones.exit_criterion: The descent theorem rules out non-trivial cycles and divergent branches.

milestones.title: Independent kernel review
milestones.exit_criterion: A clean Lean replay passes outside PrimeProject.

proof_milestone_queue.promotion_rule: Only complete milestones backed by replayable artifacts can reduce the open_count. Heuristic evidence cannot close a milestone.

decisive_lemma_lab.decisive_question: Can the bounded trajectory evidence be compressed into a finite symbolic descent cover for all residue blocks?
decisive_lemma_lab.candidate_statement: There is a finite set of accelerated Collatz residue-block certificates whose recursive lift forces every positive integer into a smaller covered representative.
decisive_lemma_lab.proof_obligation: Prove the block cover is complete under lifting and that its descent measure is well-founded, excluding cycles and divergence.
decisive_lemma_lab.falsification_test: Find a residue block with no certified descent path or a lifted block whose measure can increase indefinitely.

automated_falsification_probe.pass_condition: every committed start reaches 1 under replay
automated_falsification_probe.proof_gap: A replay through the limit does not produce a symbolic residue-block cover or a well-founded descent proof.

gaps.description: Finite trajectories must be replaced by a complete symbolic residue-block cover.
gaps.next_experiment: Generate odd-only residue transition blocks and rank uncovered classes by shortest missing descent.
gaps.failure_signal: A residue block remains uncovered or branches into a non-descending class under every tested modulus.

gaps.description: The cover must provide a well-founded measure that cannot increase forever.
gaps.next_experiment: Search for a monotone block measure that decreases across the generated transition cover.
gaps.failure_signal: The measure admits an infinite non-decreasing path or requires probabilistic drift assumptions.

gaps.description: The descent theorem must exclude both non-trivial cycles and divergent branches.
gaps.next_experiment: Derive cycle and divergence exclusions from the same well-founded measure instead of separate empirical checks.
gaps.failure_signal: A non-trivial cycle or divergent branch remains compatible with the descent cover.

gaps.description: A clean replay must verify the accelerated-map equivalence and descent proof.
gaps.next_experiment: Export the residue cover and descent theorem to a clean proof-assistant replay package.
gaps.failure_signal: The replay depends on PrimeProject-specific generated state or unchecked trajectory assumptions.

proof_gap_taxonomy.closure_rule: All proof gaps must be closed by formal proof artifacts or accepted theorem references before the conjecture page can change status.

decisive_lemma_lab.current_result: The bounded replay supports the search for descent certificates but still depends on finite enumeration.
decisive_lemma_lab.next_action: Generate candidate odd-only residue transitions and rank uncovered blocks by descent failure risk.
decisive_lemma_lab.promotion_rule: The lab may upgrade a milestone only when the proof obligation is discharged by a formal theorem or accepted external proof, not by finite probe success.

problems.claim_boundary: No proof claim. Current evidence is finite exhaustive replay plus blocker list.

proof_status_gate.machine_rule: A full-proof claim is blocked unless certificate status is bounded_theorem_certified and every obligation, graph link, theorem bridge, and lemma candidate is formal_proof_verified or accepted_theorem.

proof_execution_protocol.current_frontier: Construct a symbolic residue-block descent certificate that covers an infinite family, not only enumerated starts.
proof_execution_protocol.primary_next_experiment: Generate odd-only residue transition blocks and rank uncovered classes by shortest missing descent.
proof_execution_protocol.primary_failure_signal: A residue block remains uncovered or branches into a non-descending class under every tested modulus.

problems.title: Goldbach Conjecture
problems.target_statement: Every even integer greater than 2 is the sum of two primes.
problems.tool_position: PrimeProject can verify every even value up to a chosen public limit and expose the hardest bounded cases; this is not an infinite proof.

certificate.statement: Every even integer 4 <= n <= 1000000 has a displayed prime-pair witness.
certificate.leaf_encoding: utf8 text, sha256 leaf, pairwise sha256 Merkle chunks

proof_attempt.attack_route: Exhaustive bounded witnesses -> thinnest residue-class model -> explicit positive lower bound for all even n.

nodes.label: Every even n > 2 has p+q witness

known_theorem_bridges.name: Circle-method lower bounds
known_theorem_bridges.role: Supply explicit positive representation counts for all large even integers.

known_theorem_bridges.name: Finite verification threshold bridge
known_theorem_bridges.role: Ensure the analytic threshold does not start above the certified finite range.

lemma_candidates.statement: For every even n >= N, the Goldbach representation count R(n) is strictly positive with explicit N <= certificate_limit.
lemma_candidates.evidence: Bounded witness search shows no failures and records hardest smallest-prime decompositions.
lemma_candidates.required_upgrade: Derive explicit constants that beat all singular-series and error-term losses.

obligations.claim: Every even n <= 1,000,000 has a prime-pair witness.
obligations.failure_mode: A witness leaf changes or an even n has no pair.

obligations.claim: Prove a positive representation-count lower bound for all even n above a threshold.
obligations.failure_mode: A thin residue class has no proven positive lower bound.

obligations.claim: Bridge the analytic threshold to the bounded certified range with no gap.
obligations.failure_mode: The analytic theorem starts above the certified range.

falsification_targets: A proposed lower bound becomes non-positive for a residue class.
The threshold exceeds the certified finite range.
The model assumes unproved prime-pair independence.

next_formalization_target.statement: For every even integer n > 2, there exist primes p and q such that n = p + q.

proof_attempt.promotion_rule: A page may move from open_not_proven only when every open obligation is replaced by an independently checkable proof that does not depend on the search limit.

proof_gates: Control prime coverage in every residue class strongly enough for all even integers.
Bridge from verified finite range to an analytic theorem for the infinite tail.
State explicit constants and thresholds so no unverified gap remains.
candidate_strategy: Use residue-drift tools to detect classes where representations are thinnest.
Compare bounded failures against Hardy-Littlewood-style expected representation counts.
Escalate only if the thin-class model yields a provable lower bound above zero.

formal_proof_contract.lean_statement: theorem primeproject_goldbach_conjecture : forall n : Nat, Even n -> 2 < n -> exists p q : Nat, Nat.Prime p /\ Nat.Prime q /\ n = p + q := by
required_artifacts: formal threshold bridge below certificate limit
forbidden_assumptions: HardyLittlewood prime-pair independence as an axiom
any axiom asserting positive Goldbach representation counts
acceptance_rules: No `sorry`, `admit`, unchecked axiom, or imported theorem equivalent to the target conjecture.
Every finite computation must be represented by a bounded certificate and every infinite step by a formal theorem.
The proof-status gate must report eligible_for_independent_review before any page can change from open_not_proven.
An external reviewer must be able to replay the kernel check without trusting PrimeProject code.
formal_proof_contract.current_blocker: open infinite obligations remain outside the proof assistant kernel

proof_milestone_queue.decisive_next_task: Prove an explicit positive Goldbach representation lower bound whose threshold is below the certified finite range.

milestones.title: Bounded witness certificate
milestones.exit_criterion: Every even number through the committed limit has a replayed prime-pair witness.

milestones.title: Lean definitions for prime-pair representations
milestones.exit_criterion: Representation-count definitions compile and match bounded witnesses.

milestones.title: Positive representation lower bound
milestones.exit_criterion: For all even n >= N, R(n) is formally proved positive.

milestones.title: Threshold bridge
milestones.exit_criterion: The analytic threshold N is at or below the certified finite limit.

milestones.title: Independent kernel review
milestones.exit_criterion: A clean Lean replay passes outside PrimeProject.

proof_milestone_queue.promotion_rule: Only complete milestones backed by replayable artifacts can reduce the open_count. Heuristic evidence cannot close a milestone.

decisive_lemma_lab.decisive_question: Can the thinnest bounded representation classes be turned into an explicit positive lower bound for all large even integers?
decisive_lemma_lab.candidate_statement: For every even n >= N, the Goldbach representation count R(n) is positive, with an explicit threshold N no larger than the committed certificate limit.
decisive_lemma_lab.proof_obligation: Derive explicit constants for a positive representation-count lower bound and bridge the analytic threshold to the certified finite range.
decisive_lemma_lab.falsification_test: Reject any proposed lower bound that becomes non-positive in a residue class or whose threshold exceeds the certificate limit.

automated_falsification_probe.pass_condition: every committed even integer has a displayed prime-pair witness
automated_falsification_probe.proof_gap: Witnesses through the limit do not give an explicit positive lower bound for all large even integers.

gaps.description: The representation count must be proved positive for every sufficiently large even integer.
gaps.next_experiment: Stress the thinnest bounded residue classes and derive candidate constants for an explicit lower-bound inequality.
gaps.failure_signal: A residue class makes the lower bound non-positive or the constants depend on unproved independence.

gaps.description: The analytic threshold must be at or below the certified bounded range.
gaps.next_experiment: Compare every candidate analytic threshold against the committed certificate limit and record the uncovered interval.
gaps.failure_signal: The threshold remains above the certified range or leaves an unchecked finite gap.

gaps.description: The proof must not assume unproved prime-pair independence or Hardy-Littlewood strength.
gaps.next_experiment: Audit every lower-bound step for hidden Hardy-Littlewood or prime-pair independence assumptions.
gaps.failure_signal: A required step is only heuristic or imports a conjecture equivalent to positive representation counts.

gaps.description: An external replay must verify the lower-bound and threshold bridge.
gaps.next_experiment: Bundle the witness certificate, threshold bridge, and assumption audit for independent replay.
gaps.failure_signal: The reviewer cannot reproduce the threshold bridge from public artifacts.

proof_gap_taxonomy.closure_rule: All proof gaps must be closed by formal proof artifacts or accepted theorem references before the conjecture page can change status.

decisive_lemma_lab.current_result: The bounded witness certificate closes the finite range only; the positive infinite lower bound is open.
decisive_lemma_lab.next_action: Use the hardest bounded decompositions to prioritize residue classes for explicit lower-bound stress tests.
decisive_lemma_lab.promotion_rule: The lab may upgrade a milestone only when the proof obligation is discharged by a formal theorem or accepted external proof, not by finite probe success.

proof_execution_protocol.current_frontier: Prove an explicit positive Goldbach representation lower bound whose threshold is below the certified finite range.
proof_execution_protocol.primary_next_experiment: Stress the thinnest bounded residue classes and derive candidate constants for an explicit lower-bound inequality.
proof_execution_protocol.primary_failure_signal: A residue class makes the lower bound non-positive or the constants depend on unproved independence.

problems.title: Twin Prime Conjecture
problems.target_statement: There are infinitely many prime pairs p and p+2.
problems.tool_position: PrimeProject can count twin pairs and compare them with heuristic density curves over finite ranges; unbounded infinitude remains the missing proof step.

certificate.statement: All twin prime pairs p,p+2 with p+2 <= 1000000 are counted by the sieve scan.
certificate.leaf_encoding: utf8 text, sha256 leaf, pairwise sha256 Merkle chunks

proof_attempt.attack_route: Certified finite twin pairs -> admissible two-point pattern analysis -> lower-bound theorem for exact gap 2.

known_theorem_bridges.name: Bounded prime gaps
known_theorem_bridges.role: Existing methods prove some bounded gaps, but not exact gap 2.

known_theorem_bridges.name: Hardy-Littlewood k-tuple heuristic
known_theorem_bridges.role: Predicts the right density but cannot be used as an assumption-free proof.

lemma_candidates.statement: There is an explicit c > 0 such that pi_2(x) >= c x / log(x)^2 for arbitrarily large x without k-tuple assumptions.
lemma_candidates.evidence: Bounded counts track the Hardy-Littlewood scale through the committed limit.
lemma_candidates.required_upgrade: Replace the heuristic density curve with an unconditional lower-bound theorem for exact gap 2.

obligations.claim: Every twin prime pair p,p+2 with p+2 <= 1,000,000 is counted by the sieve scan.
obligations.failure_mode: The pair list Merkle root changes or a pair is missed.

obligations.claim: Prove infinitely many exact prime gaps of size 2, not only bounded gaps.
obligations.failure_mode: The proof only gives a bounded gap larger than 2.

obligations.claim: Remove dependence on unproved Hardy-Littlewood k-tuple assumptions.

falsification_targets: The lower bound collapses to zero for exact gap 2.
The argument proves bounded gaps but cannot force gap 2.
An admissible-pattern step assumes the k-tuple conjecture.

next_formalization_target.statement: For infinitely many primes p, p + 2 is also prime.

proof_attempt.promotion_rule: A page may move from open_not_proven only when every open obligation is replaced by an independently checkable proof that does not depend on the search limit.

proof_gates: Prove infinitely many prime gaps of size exactly 2, not merely bounded gaps.
Remove dependence on unproven distribution assumptions such as full Hardy-Littlewood k-tuple strength.
Turn finite density persistence into a lower-bound theorem for all large x.
candidate_strategy: Use wheel/residue drift measurements to isolate admissible two-point patterns.
Stress-test whether observed twin density survives generator and modulus changes.
Treat density agreement as a guide for lemma search, not as proof.

formal_proof_contract.lean_statement: theorem primeproject_twin_prime_conjecture : forall N : Nat, exists p : Nat, N < p /\ Nat.Prime p /\ Nat.Prime (p + 2) := by
forbidden_assumptions: HardyLittlewood k-tuple conjecture as an axiom
bounded gaps theorem treated as exact gap 2
acceptance_rules: No `sorry`, `admit`, unchecked axiom, or imported theorem equivalent to the target conjecture.
Every finite computation must be represented by a bounded certificate and every infinite step by a formal theorem.
The proof-status gate must report eligible_for_independent_review before any page can change from open_not_proven.
An external reviewer must be able to replay the kernel check without trusting PrimeProject code.
formal_proof_contract.current_blocker: open infinite obligations remain outside the proof assistant kernel

proof_milestone_queue.decisive_next_task: Replace Hardy-Littlewood-scale agreement with an unconditional exact gap-2 lower-bound theorem.

milestones.title: Bounded twin-pair certificate
milestones.exit_criterion: The committed sieve replay reproduces every twin pair through the limit.

milestones.title: Lean definitions for exact gap-2 primes
milestones.exit_criterion: Exact gap-2 and twin-count definitions compile without heuristic assumptions.

milestones.title: Unconditional exact gap-2 lower bound
milestones.exit_criterion: A positive lower bound for exact twin pairs is proved for arbitrarily large x.

milestones.title: Infinitude bridge
milestones.exit_criterion: The lower bound implies arbitrarily large twin prime pairs.

milestones.title: Independent kernel review
milestones.exit_criterion: A clean Lean replay passes outside PrimeProject.

proof_milestone_queue.promotion_rule: Only complete milestones backed by replayable artifacts can reduce the open_count. Heuristic evidence cannot close a milestone.

decisive_lemma_lab.decisive_question: Can observed exact gap-2 persistence be upgraded into an unconditional lower bound for arbitrarily large x?
decisive_lemma_lab.candidate_statement: There is an explicit c > 0 such that the number of twin prime pairs up to x is at least c x / log(x)^2 for arbitrarily large x, without assuming Hardy-Littlewood.
decisive_lemma_lab.proof_obligation: Prove an unconditional exact gap-2 lower bound strong enough to imply arbitrarily large twin prime pairs.
decisive_lemma_lab.falsification_test: Reject any argument that proves only bounded gaps, assumes k-tuple independence, or lets the exact gap-2 lower bound collapse to zero.

automated_falsification_probe.pass_condition: observed twin-pair count remains positive at every committed checkpoint
automated_falsification_probe.proof_gap: Positive bounded counts and heuristic-scale agreement do not prove infinitely many exact gap-2 pairs.

gaps.description: A positive lower bound is needed for exact gap-2 pairs, not merely bounded prime gaps.
gaps.next_experiment: Separate exact gap-2 counts from bounded-gap signals and test candidate lower-bound forms against wheel/residue stress cases.
gaps.failure_signal: The argument proves only bounded gaps larger than 2 or the exact gap-2 lower bound collapses to zero.

gaps.description: Hardy-Littlewood k-tuple behavior cannot be used as an assumption.
gaps.next_experiment: Identify which observed density features are heuristic-only and remove them from the formal target statement.
gaps.failure_signal: The proof still requires k-tuple independence or Hardy-Littlewood-scale density as an axiom.

gaps.description: The exact gap-2 lower bound must imply arbitrarily large twin pairs.
gaps.next_experiment: Formalize the implication from a positive exact-gap lower bound over arbitrarily large x to infinitely many twin primes.
gaps.failure_signal: The lower bound applies only to bounded ranges or averaged windows without forcing arbitrarily large pairs.

gaps.description: A clean external replay must verify exact gap-2, not just bounded gaps.
gaps.next_experiment: Prepare a minimal external replay package that distinguishes exact gap 2 from bounded-gap theorems.
gaps.failure_signal: The replay verifies a bounded-gap theorem but not exact twin-prime infinitude.

proof_gap_taxonomy.closure_rule: All proof gaps must be closed by formal proof artifacts or accepted theorem references before the conjecture page can change status.

decisive_lemma_lab.current_result: The bounded count agrees with heuristic scale but does not establish infinitude.
decisive_lemma_lab.next_action: Separate exact gap-2 requirements from bounded-gap evidence and formalize the missing lower-bound theorem.
decisive_lemma_lab.promotion_rule: The lab may upgrade a milestone only when the proof obligation is discharged by a formal theorem or accepted external proof, not by finite probe success.

problems.claim_boundary: No proof claim. Current evidence is finite counting and heuristic comparison.

proof_status_gate.machine_rule: A full-proof claim is blocked unless certificate status is bounded_theorem_certified and every obligation, graph link, theorem bridge, and lemma candidate is formal_proof_verified or accepted_theorem.

proof_execution_protocol.current_frontier: Replace Hardy-Littlewood-scale agreement with an unconditional exact gap-2 lower-bound theorem.
proof_execution_protocol.primary_next_experiment: Separate exact gap-2 counts from bounded-gap signals and test candidate lower-bound forms against wheel/residue stress cases.
proof_execution_protocol.primary_failure_signal: The argument proves only bounded gaps larger than 2 or the exact gap-2 lower bound collapses to zero.

stages.name: bounded certificate replay
stages.input: finite computation rows
stages.proof_value: Closes only the finite range; it does not discharge an infinite conjecture.

stages.name: formal target normalization
stages.input: Lean-oriented theorem statement and forbidden assumptions
stages.required_output: kernel-checkable definitions with no sorry/admit/target-equivalent axiom
stages.proof_value: Prevents finite evidence or conjectural imports from being mistaken for proof.

stages.name: decisive lemma attack
stages.input: There is an explicit c > 0 such that the number of twin prime pairs up to x is at least c x / log(x)^2 for arbitrarily large x, without assuming Hardy-Littlewood.
stages.required_output: Prove an unconditional exact gap-2 lower bound strong enough to imply arbitrarily large twin prime pairs.
stages.verifier: formal theorem or accepted external theorem
stages.proof_value: The first infinite step that would close the main open milestones.

stages.name: gap work-order falsification
stages.input: Separate exact gap-2 counts from bounded-gap signals and test candidate lower-bound forms against wheel/residue stress cases.
stages.verifier: The argument proves only bounded gaps larger than 2 or the exact gap-2 lower bound collapses to zero.
stages.proof_value: Turns the next research move into a testable success/failure condition.

stages.name: full proof promotion gate
stages.input: all obligations, graph links, theorem bridges, lemma candidates, and formal contract
stages.proof_value: Blocks any public full-proof claim until every infinite bridge is independently checkable.

proof_execution_protocol.promotion_rule: The conjecture page cannot claim solved status until every protocol stage is complete or formally verified and the proof-status gate is eligible_for_independent_review.
